places.details: The heart of Manali, ideal for a leisurely stroll, shopping for local handicrafts, woolen clothes, and enjoying street food.
Famous for its picturesque landscapes and adventure sports like paragliding, zorbing, and skiing (in winter). Enjoy stunning mountain views.

Enjoy the serenity and natural beauty along the banks of the Beas River. You can opt for riverside picnics or simply relax by the water.

places.details: Experience a more laid-back vibe with quaint cafes, guesthouses, and local shops. Great for soaking in the bohemian atmosphere.

A moderate trek through pine trees and orchards leads to these beautiful waterfalls. A refreshing experience.

places.details: A public beach known for its clean sands and clear waters, perfect for a relaxing start to your Maldives trip.

places.place_name: Snorkeling Trip (e.g., Banana Reef or Biyadhoo)
places.details: Explore vibrant coral reefs teeming with diverse marine life. Banana Reef is known for its rich biodiversity, while Biyadhoo is a popular spot for snorkelers and divers.
places.ticket_pricing: Varies (around $30-$50 USD for guided tours, can be cheaper if booked locally)
places.travel_time_from_previous_location: 30 minutes (from a local island like Maafushi via speedboat)
places.best_time_to_visit: Morning (9 AM - 12 PM) for best visibility

places.details: Relax on the shores of a local island, enjoying the sun and sea after your snorkeling adventure. Many local islands have designated 'bikini beaches' for tourists.

places.details: Embark on a traditional Maldivian Dhoni boat to witness a breathtaking sunset over the Indian Ocean. Keep an eye out for dolphins!

places.travel_time_from_previous_location: 1 hour (by public ferry from Maafushi to Male)

places.place_name: Huvafen Fushi (Resort View from public areas)
places.details: While not staying at this luxury resort, you can admire its beauty from public viewpoints or if you arrange a day pass (which might exceed a cheap budget). Focus on enjoying the general scenery.

places.details: The most iconic landmark of Paris, offering breathtaking panoramic views of the city. Consider walking up to the second floor for a more active and cheaper experience.

places.best_time_to_visit: Early morning or late evening to avoid crowds and for beautiful light

places.details: Home to thousands of works of art, including the Mona Lisa and Venus de Milo. Focus on key exhibits to save time and energy.

places.details: A beautiful public garden situated between the Louvre Museum and the Place de la Concorde, perfect for a leisurely stroll.

places.details: While still under restoration, admire the exterior of this magnificent Gothic cathedral from the surrounding area.

places.best_time_to_visit: Morning to enjoy cooler temperatures and fewer crowds

places.details: Wander through the historic Latin Quarter, known for its lively atmosphere, student population, bookstores, and charming streets. Visit the Panthéon from the outside.

places.details: Enjoy a relaxing and scenic boat tour along the Seine River, offering beautiful views of Parisian landmarks from a different perspective. Look for basic 1-hour tours.
